message:
feat(spec-explorer,glow-orbits): replace DOMPurify HTML rendering with React AST renderer, fix hydration mismatch

Spec Explorer (spec-viewer.tsx):
Replace the DOMPurify-based dangerouslySetInnerHTML rendering pipeline
with a fully React-native AST-based markdown renderer. The old approach
parsed markdown to HTML, sanitized it with DOMPurify, and injected it
as raw HTML -- which prevented React-level interactivity, broke
accessibility, and made inter-document linking impossible. The new
renderer walks marked's token AST directly and produces React elements,
enabling:
- Per-section color tones with accent borders and halo backgrounds
- Code block copy-to-clipboard buttons
- Inter-document link resolution (clicking a .md reference navigates
  within the spec explorer instead of triggering a page load)
- Section-level navigation chips with heading icons
- Structured document stats (word count, read time, code/table/link
  counts)
- Proper React key management and type safety throughout

The DOMPurify dependency is no longer imported by this component. New
type definitions (MarkdownToken, ParsedDocSection, SectionTone, etc.)
replace the old stringly-typed HTML pipeline. The loadSpecDocHtml
function is renamed to loadSpecDocSource since it now returns raw
markdown instead of sanitized HTML.

GlowOrbits (glow-orbits.tsx):
Fix a Next.js hydration mismatch caused by the parallax motion styles
being applied during SSR when window dimensions are unavailable. The
server renders with motion values at 0,0 while the client immediately
computes different values from actual viewport dimensions, causing a
React hydration error. The fix introduces a `parallaxReady` state that
starts false and only becomes true after a useEffect initializes the
mouse position to the viewport center. The motion style prop is gated
on this state, ensuring server and client render identical initial
markup.

CSS (globals.css):
Remove the entire "Spec Explorer Prose" CSS section (~174 lines) that
styled the old DOMPurify HTML output. These styles are no longer needed
because the new React AST renderer applies Tailwind classes directly to
each element. The section numbering is updated accordingly (Component
Utilities moves from section 8 to section 7).

E2E (smoke.spec.ts):
Update the spec explorer smoke test selector from `.spec-prose:visible`
to `[data-spec-doc-body="true"]:visible` to match the new React
renderer's data attribute instead of the removed CSS class.

Beads (issues.jsonl):
Track new mobile rendering stabilization work: issue bd-2gc (GlowOrbits
hydration fix) moves to in_progress, and new issues bd-ilq (epic),
bd-3ou (mobile Playwright regression coverage), and bd-3vj (end-to-end
validation) are created to track the broader mobile stabilization
effort.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
